name: zstdlib

metadata.name: zstdlib
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: A set of useful python utilities

documentation:
# zstdlib
A set of useful python utilities

functions.name: zstdlib.frozen.frozen
documentation:
A class decorator to permanently freeze a class after some method, __init__ by default
If passed a string, will freeze after the method with that name

classes.name: zstdlib.frozen.Freezable

methods.name: zstdlib.frozen.Freezable.freeze
documentation: Prevent further modifications to this object

methods.name: zstdlib.frozen.Freezable.thaw
documentation: Allow modifications to this object
A base class for objects that can be frozen

functions.name: zstdlib.log.trace.install
documentation:
Install TRACE, logging.trace, getLogger().trace, etc into the logging module
\:param value: The TRACE log level; default: logging.DEBUG // 2
\:param force: If true, force install most errors

functions.name: zstdlib.log.trace._define_logger_trace
documentation: Helpers

class_variables.name: zstdlib.log.trace._State.__init__
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: zstdlib.not_set.NotSetType
documentation:
A type that exists to denote a value has not been set
This might be useful when None is a valid value for a type

classes.name: zstdlib.enum.EnumType

methods.name: zstdlib.enum.EnumType.__delattr__
documentation: Disallow modification

Metaclass for uninstantiable Enum classes with required annotations and unique values
Enum values may not be prefixed with _
These 'Enum' classes may not be modified after creation

classes.name: zstdlib.enum.Enum
documentation:
A uninstantiable Enum base type
Subclasses must provide type-annotated fields with unique values
Fields may not be prefixed with "_"
Derived classes will not be modifiable

classes.name: zstdlib.log.cute.CuteFormatter

methods.name: zstdlib.log.cute.CuteFormatter.__init__
documentation:
log_colors may be overridden, for example, trace logs are printed dimly
Do not modify [cute] parameters in the fmt string; they might have special formatting that is not visible
Ex. %(cute_name)-8s should be done via cute_widths\["cute_name"\] = 8
This is because cute_widths takes into account 0-width color codes when padding
Level colors used are: DEFAULT_LEVEL_COLORS | level_colors
Dimming of log messages is done for levels \< dim_level
\:param fmt: The format string to use for the log message; do not modify [cute] parameters
\:param args: Passed to logging.Formatter
\:param colored: If False, no colors will be used regardless of the colors parameter
\:param colors: The colors to use for the given loggers, automatic for non-specified loggers
\:param level_colors: The colors to use >= the given levels, automatic for non-specified loggers
\:param cute_widths: The widths of the [cute] columns in the log message
\:param kwargs: Passed to logging.Formatter
pylint: disable=dangerous-default-value,keyword-arg-before-vararg,too-many-arguments

methods.name: zstdlib.log.cute.CuteFormatter.update
documentation: Set the colors for all loggers; enables colors if disabled
A log formatter that can print log messages with colors.

functions.name: zstdlib.io.io
documentation:
Convert an IO object to a TextIO or BinaryIO object
    For raw to be valid, one of the three must be true:
    1. raw is an io.TextIOBase, io.RawIOBase, or io.BufferedIOBase object
    2. raw has a .mode string attribute
    3. binary must be set
If the three detection methods disagree on if raw is binary, a TypeError is raised

classes.name: zstdlib.io.ProtectedFile

methods.name: zstdlib.io.ProtectedFile.__init__
documentation:
---
param raw: The raw IO object to wrap, users can access it directly if they must
---

methods.name: zstdlib.io.ProtectedFile.__getattr__
documentation: Prevent access to certain attributes

A wrapper around an IO object that prevents access to certain attributes
If absolutely necessary, users can access the raw object directly via .raw

classes.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.__new__
documentation: Create a new instance of cls for raw, or return an existing instance if one exists

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the IO object with a raw IO object
Note: This class takes ownership of the input object, do not use it elsewhere
\:param f: The raw IO object to wrap
\:param binary: If True, the IO object will read/write bytes, otherwise it will read/write strings

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.read
documentation:
Read up to size characters from self.raw or until EOF
If size is unspecified or -1, read until EOF

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.readline
documentation:
Read a line from self.raw or until EOF
Newline is included in the return value

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.readlines
documentation: Read all lines from self.raw until EOF

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.peek
documentation: Read size characters from self.raw or until EOF, without consuming the data

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.unread
documentation: Unread data back to the internal buffer

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.readuntil
documentation:
Read until until is found in f. If eof_ok is False, raise EOFError if until is not found before EOF.
\:param until: The str/bytes to read until
\:param inclusive: If True, the returned value will end with until
\:param eof_ok: if False, raise EOFError if until is not found before EOF
\:return: The data read from self.raw, up to and including until

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase._read
documentation: Helper functions

methods.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.__hash__
documentation: Protection

class_variables.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.__deepcopy__
documentation: type: ignore

class_variables.name: zstdlib.io._IOWrapperBase.__copy__
documentation: type: ignore

A base class for IO objects that adds additional functionality such as readuntil, unread, and peek
These classes are singletons with respect to their input object
Note: This class takes ownership of the input object, do not use it elsewhere
See bug: https://github.com/pylint-dev/pylint/issues/9335
pylint: disable=undefined-variable

classes.name: zstdlib.io.TextIO
documentation: A wrapper around TextIO objects that add additional functionality such as readuntil, unread, and peek

classes.name: zstdlib.io.BinaryIO
documentation: A wrapper around BinaryIO objects that add additional functionality such as readuntil, unread, and peek

Technically this is more of a heuristic, but it failing is extremely unlikely

methods.name: tests.log.test_trace.TestTrace.test_trace

Avoid splitting up into multiple functions since trace.install() affects global state
Keeping this as one function ensures that the tests are run in order

methods.name: zstdlib.ansi.Color.factory
documentation:
---
return: The Ansi given the chosen color and various modifiers
---

Represents an Ansi color code; calling this on a string will apply the code to the string
Can be constructed via .factory; alternatively calling Color.<name> or Color(<name>) will
automatically construct a color. Colors are constructed with attributes determined by
splitting <name> on "_". Colors may be made bright by prepending "bright_" to the color name
Background colors may be specified by prepending "BG_" to a color name
    BG_ must precede bright_ if both are specified
Foreground and background colors may both be specified at once
Examples:
    Color.red
    Color.default
    Color.BRIGHT_BLUE
    Color.BG_green
    Color.Bright_BG_black
    Color.bold_underline_yellow
    Color.bold_bright_bg_BLUE_bright_green
    Color.BOLD_bright_Red_strikethrough_bg_Bright_GREEN
